summary: Chromium cookie extractor with App-Bound Encryption bypass; offensive tooling published under placeholder identity
details: Package implements a Chromium User Data extractor targeting Chrome, Edge, and Brave: it locates the browser profile, DPAPI-decrypts the master key from Local State, attaches a debugger to the browser process to recover the v20 App-Bound Encryption AES key via a native addon (addon.findOffset / addon.extractKey), decrypts the cookie database, and writes the results to./<browser>_cookies/*.txt in Netscape format. The capability fires only when the installer explicitly invokes the CLI or API — there is no install-time or import-time hostile action (the only lifecycle behavior is node-gyp rebuild against shipped C++ sources), no network I/O, and no covert destination receiving extracted cookies. Publisher metadata is unfilled: repository/bugs/homepage URLs all point to the literal placeholder github.com/yourusername/baksen, and binding.gyp hardcodes the author's local vcpkg paths (C:/Users/ibrahim/vcpkg/...) which will break node-gyp on any other machine. The package is functionally an infostealer primitive (browser session theft is exactly the harm class this engine treats as critical when wired to exfiltration), but in this version that primitive is not wired to any attacker-controlled sink — invoking it is the installer's own choice. Routing to human review given the dual-use nature, the placeholder publisher identity, and the build configuration that prevents normal installation from succeeding.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
